title: PraxecPacksRegistry
description: v0.0.17 tool-source ecosystem contract (D4b). The `praxec.packs/v3` registry — a compatible superset of ADR-0013 `praxec.packs/v2`: the same `packs` + `tools` catalog, where each tool MAY additionally carry a D1 tool descriptor (`descriptor`, $ref tool-descriptor.schema.json) and `suggested_workflows`, plus an optional top-level `crossmatrix` (tool x workflow topology index). The registry describes what a pack offers; it never grants — activation stays an operator `grant_connections:` act (D3).

crossmatrix.description: Registry-level topology index: which workflows compose which tools. DERIVED from descriptors + workflow step refs, never hand-maintained (FM7); every row must reference a tool id and a suggested workflow definitionId present in this registry.

namespace.description: The `repos:` namespace the pack layers under (V20 uniqueness).

repo.description: Git URL of the pack repo.

requires.description: Tool ids (from this registry's `tools`) the pack's workflows depend on.

external.description: Tool ids provisioned outside this registry (not resolvable here by design).

extends.description: Optional parent pack id this pack layers on top of.

command.description: The binary the tool's connection spawns (v2 mcp coordinate; also valid for cli tools). A rest descriptor conflicts with this (REGISTRY_TOOL_KIND_CONFLICT).

mcp_registry_id.description: ADR-0013 MCP registry id, e.g. io.github.praxec/cpm-planner. mcp-only: a cli/rest descriptor conflicts with this (REGISTRY_TOOL_KIND_CONFLICT).

providers.description: ADR-0013 provider chain, provider -> coordinate (image / URL / crate). Doctor offers with consent, never auto-installs (FM5).

descriptor.description: v3: the D1 tool descriptor — lets the registry describe cli and rest tools, not just mcp. Its `kind` must not conflict with the v2 coordinates above.

suggested_workflows.description: v3: workflow definitionIds (namespace-qualified) that compose this tool.

tool.description: A tool id present in this registry's `tools`.

workflow.description: A workflow definitionId suggested somewhere in this registry (tool-level or descriptor-level `suggested_workflows`).

role.description: Provenance of the edge: `suggested` is derived from the tool's suggested_workflows; `dependency` is derived from the workflow's step executor.connection refs (FM7: derived, never hand-maintained).
